sentences.english: Thank you for taking the time to meet with me, I really value your guidance.
sentences.korean: 시간 내주셔서 감사합니다. 선배님의 조언을 정말 소중히 여깁니다.

sentences.english: Of course. What's on your mind?
I've been at my current company for three years and I'm not sure if I should stay or look for something new.
sentences.korean: 현재 회사에 3년째인데 계속 있어야 할지 새 곳을 찾아야 할지 모르겠어요.
떠나는 걸 고려하게 하는 게 뭔가요?

sentences.english: I feel like my growth has plateaued.

quiz.question: What does '의견을 말하다' mean in English?

sentences.english: I'm doing the same tasks I was doing a year ago and there's no clear path to promotion.
sentences.korean: 1년 전과 같은 업무를 하고 있고 승진 경로가 명확하지 않아요.

sentences.english: Have you had an honest conversation with your manager about your career aspirations?
sentences.korean: 상사에게 커리어 포부에 대해 솔직하게 이야기해보셨나요?

sentences.english: I'd start there before making any big decisions.
sentences.korean: 큰 결정을 내리기 전에 거기서부터 시작하는 게 좋겠어요.

sentences.english: Sometimes managers don't realize their team members want more responsibility unless they speak up.
sentences.korean: 가끔 상사들은 팀원이 더 많은 책임을 원한다는 걸 직접 말하지 않으면 모르거든요.
